nodes.text: Faust wakes at four a.m. with the sound of an alarm.
If it is 4am, then I am awake.
He pulls a tug of water to drink and gets up out of bed.
Before this waking I had lucid dreamt a dream in which I'd had riches — as a captain of industry, movie star debonair. How peculiar.
Faust looks in the mirror at his early-morning haggard.
At least it wasn't one in which my teeth all fall out and death is imminent. Maybe tomorrow.
He opens the mirror to get his vitamins. He takes one with a tug of water.
IMAGES: Faust clubbing with two young girls. The Robocop music video montage of Detroit thumps in the background.
These modern draughts, my learned trade — I down you with OCP and OTC. Yeah, you know me: your legal and commercial medicinal liaison.
Cut back. Faust puts on his medical blue uniform and white jacket and heads to work.
Not so heavily medicated just yet, still feeling the effects of last night — though an education away from exactly who I need to be.
Regret not loving enough, not living enough — outside my head.
